_meta.purpose: Stable, human-readable facts about the Tangible Careers Linear workspace. The tangible-linear and linear-organize skills read this FIRST, then resolve live IDs and volatile lists via the Linear MCP.
rules: Names only — never store Linear IDs here (they change when objects are recreated; resolve them live with list_teams / list_issue_labels / list_initiatives).
Treat every list below as the canonical *convention*. For current *state* (which projects/issues exist right now, which sprint labels are created), always query Linear live.
When this file and the live workspace disagree, prefer live for state and this file for convention — and flag the drift to a human (see needsHumanDecision).
_meta.seededBy: audit via Linear MCP (list_teams, list_users, list_issue_labels, list_initiatives, list_projects)

teams.name: Engineering
teams.note: Builds/maintains the platform. Full 2-week Scrum, rotating scrum master.

teams.name: AI Research
teams.note: Lightweight backlog, no strict sprints. Engineering depends on its deliverables (e.g. Skill Map v1).

teams.name: Business/Company
teams.note: Client / business-owner work and company projects.

teams.name: Tangible Founder
teams.note: Founder-led programs & workshops (EU UDAAN, YI Lab, AI for Entrepreneurs). NOT documented in the original guidelines — confirm how it should be treated.

people.note: Active human members seeded from list_users. Team assignment beyond Aaryash/Kaushal is unconfirmed — see needsHumanDecision.people.
referencedButNotInLinear: Prabin — named in the docs and the ai-eval label description as an Engineering member, but has no Linear user account. Confirm/invite.

sprints.mechanism: sprint/YYYY-MM-DD issue LABELS under the `sprint/` group — NOT Linear Cycles
sprints.computeExample: python3 -c "import datetime as d; a=d.date(2026,6,15); t=d.date.today(); print('sprint/'+(a+d.timedelta(days=14*((t-a).days//14))).isoformat())"
sprints.anchorValidatedBy: live label sprint/2026-06-29 exists and matches the anchor's 14-day parity
sprints.rollover: manual — re-tag unfinished issues with the next sprint label

labels.note: Canonical issue-label taxonomy. Apply as many as genuinely fit. Lowercase, exact names. `frontend` is team-scoped (Engineering); the rest are workspace-level.

type.business-req: Client / business-owner work — external, usually deadlined.
type.engineering-goal: Committed dev work — features / planned tasks pulled into a sprint.
type.tech-debt: Refactors, cleanup, code-health — not a feature or bug.
type.bug: Defect fix — a broken behavior, not new work (engineering-goal) or cleanup (tech-debt). Canonical lowercase; migrate the live capitalized `Bug`. Pair with backend/frontend.

area.backend: Express APIs, services, Postgres, migrations, auth, AWS/pm2.
area.frontend: React UI, components, state, styling. (team-scoped: Engineering)
area.ai-eval: AI pipeline, eval prompts, scoring, AI interview, benchmarking, tuning.
area.security: authn/authz, RBAC, XSS, secrets, CSP, token handling, IDOR. ADDED from live workspace — was missing from the skill.
area.performance: render cost, store subscriptions, caching/query efficiency, bundle size. ADDED from live workspace — was missing from the skill.

state.blocked: Waiting on a dependency/decision. Pair with the blocked-by relation. Remove when unblocked.

projectLabels.feature: PROJECT label only (surfaces a project in the pm-dashboard Features column). NEVER apply to an issue. Matched case-insensitively by the board. Can only be created in Linear's UI.

liveExtrasToReconcile.note: Present in the live workspace but not in the canonical taxonomy. Decide keep / rename-to-canonical / archive — see needsHumanDecision.labels.
liveExtrasToReconcile.Feature: exists as an issue label; taxonomy says `feature` is a PROJECT label only

initiatives.note: Seeded LIVE from list_initiatives on 2026-07-23. This REPLACES the fictional table that was hard-coded in the linear-organize skill (Platform Foundations / Educator Experience Rebuild / Agentic Development Layer / AI Research & Skill Intelligence / Programs & Partnerships / Core Platform — NONE of which exist). Always re-fetch live before acting.
initiatives.rule: An initiative must be a big, finishable goal spanning several projects — never a permanent bucket.

live.name: Agents-First Platform
live.theme: Machine-legible API contract + tooling so agents can drive platform flows.

live.name: LMS Enhancement
live.theme: Richer course content + cleaner learner/educator/evaluator experiences.

live.name: Code Health & Tech-Debt Control
live.theme: Tests, validations, transactions, error handling, legacy cleanup.

live.name: Usability & Quality of Life
live.theme: UX polish and friction-reducing improvements, feedback-driven.

live.name: Public Courses & Certification
live.theme: Public course sharing + certificate enhancements (business requirement).

live.name: Course Builder POC
live.theme: Throwaway POC: prompt-driven course creation on a Neo4j skill graph.

live.name: Workshop
live.theme: Founder workshops. Sparse — confirm whether finishable or should be split/closed.

standaloneProjects.note: Intentionally live at workspace level with NO initiative.

labels: DECIDED 2026-07-23: `security`, `performance`, and lowercase `bug` are now canonical (documented only — no Linear changes made).
OPEN: migrate the live capitalized `Bug` label to lowercase `bug`.
OPEN: `Feature`/`Improvement` and the design-workflow labels (QA Review, Rubric Design, Activity Design, Content Development, Regulator Variant, Backend PR Review) — keep, rename, or archive.
people: Confirm Engineering roster: docs say Aaryash/Kaushal/Prabin. Prabin has no Linear account. Are Prekshya Dawadi / Unisa Sakha engineers or another team?
teams: How should the `Tangible Founder` team be represented in the conventions? (programs/workshops live there)
initiatives: Map the 43 projects to the 7 live initiatives — most are currently unlinked. This is the /linear-organize migration and is a human-in-the-loop step.
